ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for berry fruit cake; 'cake food product' (FOODON_03301710) is the closest defensible broad match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for berry fruit cake; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical slice of berry fruit cake weighs approximately 80 g, representing a standard single-serve portion at teatime or as a snack.

nutrient_source.title: Berry Fruit Cake — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Berry Fruit Cake (cr_id G00010809). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from analogous HPB cake entries (~4.2 g/100g) as the HPB record does not disaggregate fat fractions; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Baked Goods — Fruit Cake and Analogous Cake Products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Berry Fruit Cake in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: 'fruit cake' (GI ~65, medium category) as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Berry fruit cake shares a similar refined-flour and sugar base with moderate fruit content, supporting this analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × net carbohydrate per serving (80 g × 61.6 g/100g = 49.3 g) ÷ 100 = 32.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (27.4 g/100g) driving insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 101 mg/100g is not independently concerning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit berry fruit cake to occasional very small portions (≤40 g) because the high sugar load (27.4 g/100g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and vascular inflammation, even though the sodium content itself (101 mg/100g) is within acceptable limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 27.4 g/100g exceeds the 20 g/100g hard threshold; high carbohydrate load (61.6 g/100g) with medium-high GI (~65) causes significant postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Berry fruit cake is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to sugar content of 27.4 g/100g and a total carbohydrate load of 61.6 g/100g with a GI of approximately 65, producing a glycemic load of ~32 per standard slice; it should be avoided or consumed only in very small portions (≤20 g) on rare occasions with close blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~4.2 g/100g combined with high sugar (27.4 g/100g) and refined carbohydrate base elevates cardiovascular risk through dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation pathways
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict berry fruit cake to small occasional portions because the combination of estimated saturated fat (~4.2 g/100g), high sugar (27.4 g/100g), and refined flour contributes to dyslipidaemia and endothelial dysfunction; berries provide beneficial polyphenols but are insufficient to offset the overall pro-atherogenic profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 27.4 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold; refined wheat flour drives high glycemic load promoting systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit berry fruit cake because sugar at 27.4 g/100g exceeds the pro-inflammatory threshold of 15 g/100g and the refined flour base drives a high glycemic load; berries provide beneficial antioxidants but cannot offset the dominant inflammatory burden of the sugar and refined carbohydrate content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Berry Fruit Cake is a baked dessert cake popular in Singapore as a teatime snack, featuring a refined wheat flour sponge base enriched with mixed berries (such as blueberries, strawberries, or raspberries), sugar, butter, and eggs. The cake is typically soft and moist in texture with a sweet flavour profile balanced by the slight tartness of the berries. It is classified under the Malay cuisine tradition in Singapore and is commonly served in slices at social gatherings, celebrations, and afternoon tea settings. Per 100 g, it provides 324 kcal, 2.9 g protein, 9.1 g fat, 61.6 g carbohydrate, 27.4 g sugar, and 3.7 g dietary fibre. The high sugar and refined carbohydrate content make it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension, and the estimated saturated fat content (~4.2 g/100g) combined with high sugar raises moderate cardiovascular concern. The berries contribute antioxidants, polyphenols, and some dietary fibre, but these benefits are outweighed by the dominant sugar and refined flour base. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 65 (medium category), yielding a high glycemic load of ~32 per standard 80 g slice. Iron content is notable at 2.07 mg/100g, likely from fortified flour. This food is halal-suitable and vegetarian but contains gluten, eggs, and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics eat berry fruit cake?
How many calories in a slice of berry fruit cake?
Is berry fruit cake suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of berry fruit cake?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010809, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. Saturated fat is estimated from analogous HPB cake entries and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest fruit cake entry.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (4.2 g/100g) is estimated from analogous HPB cake entries as the HPB record for G00010809 does not disaggregate fat fractions; this value should be updated when disaggregated fat data becomes available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for Berry Fruit Cake (G00010809) and are not available in the Singapore HPB database as of 2026. 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using the 'fruit cake' entry; no direct GI measurement for berry fruit cake was found. 4. All core macros (protein_g 2.9, fat_g 9.1, carbohydrate_g 61.6) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required.
